messages.content: Angela's got the budget locked down, Pam keeps us organized and on schedule, and Kelly knows what the people actually want to have fun. That's a party trifecta.

The summer office party budget has already been approved and is on file: $400 total, allocated as Food $180, Non-alcoholic beverages $60, Decorations $95, and Miscellaneous $65. The theme is white and gold — tasteful, appropriate, and not subject to revision. There will be no DJ, no inflatable decorations, and no last-minute additions that circumvent the Party Planning Committee review process. If anyone has suggestions, they may submit them in writing for my consideration, though I will tell you now that most of them will be rejected.

Oh, a summer party — I'm already on it! Based on what worked (and what didn't) last year, I want to make sure we lock in the details before anything gets promised to the team. Here's what I need to get started: a rough headcount confirmation, a few potential dates in July or August, and a budget number from accounting — Oscar can help with that. Once I have those three things, I can put together a couple of location options for Michael to choose from and get the invites out with plenty of lead time. Should I send a quick availability poll to the office today?

Oh my god, okay, so based on how AMAZING the Golden Hour Garden Party went in June — which, by the way, everyone loved, I told Michael it was going to be a hit and he didn't listen to me at first but then he did — we should do a Late Summer Sunset theme, like deep terracotta, burnt orange, and warm gold everywhere, fairy lights obviously because they're timeless, and a grazing table with charcuterie and those little caprese skewers that look so good on Instagram. For music we're doing Beyoncé's *Renaissance*, Sabrina Carpenter, and maybe some Chappell Roan because she is having a MOMENT right now and if we don't acknowledge that we are basically saying we don't care about culture. Activities-wise, a photo corner with a ring light and some props — people go crazy for that — and maybe a "build your own agua fresca" station because hydration is very trendy right now and also Ryan mentioned he's been doing a cleanse so it's inclusive.

Okay, WOW — first of all, I just want to say that none of this would be happening without my leadership, because as Wayne Gretzky once said, "You miss 100% of the shots you don't take," and I took the shot by assembling the greatest party-planning team in Scranton, possibly the world. Here is the plan: we are working with an approved $400 budget — Food $180, Beverages $60, Decorations $95, Miscellaneous $65 — and the theme is white and gold, which I actually think is very classy and very *me*, so Angela and I are basically on the same wavelength there. Pam is going to send out an availability poll TODAY so we can lock in a July or August date, and once we have a headcount she'll put together location options for me to personally choose from, because that is a decision that requires a visionary. Kelly had some incredible ideas — the fairy lights, the grazing table, the agua fresca station — and while some of that may need to go through the Party Planning Committee review process in writing, I want Kelly to know that I *heard* her, and that is what great bosses do. This is going to be the best summer party Dunder Mifflin has ever seen, and years from now people will say, "I was there, and Michael Scott made
